If you like discs with thick wing length, you’ll love the distance you can get from the Nuke SS. The Nuke SS can be purchased in the highly durable Z-line, or premium Titanium plastics. If you want to add some style, you can get this disc in dyed plastic as well. For night play, this disc is available in Z glow.

name: Passion
description: The Passion is a Paige Pierce branded fairway driver with a straight flight and a unique flat outer rim configuration. It is very similar to the classic Discraft Cyclone but with less of a bead, tuning it for a straighter, more dependable flight for precision placement on the fairway.

name: Predator
description: The Predator is a predictable, overstable fairway driver. It is not the fastest disc on the market, but it has a reliable and consistent fade. Discraft gives this disc a stability rating of 2.5. This is the perfect disc for forehand flicks. The Predator comes in three high-quality Discraft plastics. Elite-Z is a an ultra hard plastic that will withstand brutal punishment. ESP plastic is one of the premium blends available. It is flexible, grip-able, and durable. The FLX blend is also flexible, and a favorite for playing in cold temperatures.

name: Pulse
description: The Pulse is designed to be a fast-flying disc that is good for forehand throws by average players. This is a very overstable disc that has an average sized rim compared with other distance drivers. The pulse is also a good driver for power throwers who have a lot of snap. Discraft gives it a stability rating of 1.9. You can purchase the Pulse in premium ESP plastic, one of the best all performance blends on the market.

name: Raptor
description: The Raptor is speed 9 disc with a flat top and overstable finish. It is designed to slice through the air in any weather conditions with a reliable flight. You'll find that the Raptor feels extremely comfortable as a forehand and backhand driver.

name: Rattler
description: The Rattler is a tall putter with a super thin rim. This is a slow flying stable disc (Discraft Rating 0) with a slight degree of turn and fade. If you’re looking for a reliable flying short range disc, you will be happy with the Rattler.

name: Reaper
description: The Discraft Reaper (not to be confused with the Undertaker) is a distance driver that was discontinued in 2012. The Reaper is a meathook of a driver, perfect for sweeping hyzer throws and placement shots.

name: Ringer
description: If you like firm, thin, putters that just feel good in your hand, the Discraft Ringer is for you. The Ringer is an overstable putter (Discraft Stability 1.5) with a low profile (1.8cm tall). The excellent feel this disc provides will help you gain confidence and make more putts. One of the best things about the Ringer is that it’s available in the low cost Pro-D plastic.

name: Ringer GT
description: The Ringer GT is a modified Ringer putt and approach disc. The top features a subtle groove track for grip confidence, and the disc is slightly more overstable now. The Ringer GT is an excellent disc for bold approaches and drivers, working for backhand or forehand control when a soft landing is needed.

name: Roach
description: The 2015 Ace Race disc. A straight flier with extra glide, the Roach is crafty in avoiding danger while incessantly pursuing chains. Just point and shoot. It's beadless too and feels great in the hand. Try a Roach on your next approach!

name: Scorch
description: The Scorch is an understable distance driver. It is much like a longer Avenger SS. This makes it a great distance driver that will have some turn and a decent amount of fade, providing a consistent "S" curve flight path every time you rip it.

name: Sled
description:
The Discraft Sled is a low glide putt-and-approach disc built for controlled placement and confident execution. A rolled thumb track adds grip security and comfort, while the rounded wing and nose provide a familiar hand feel similar to the Luna and Roach.

With flight numbers of 3 | 2 | -1 | 2, the Sled offers an easy high-speed flip, straight point-and-shoot control, and a gentle, dependable fade. Its slower speed and minimal glide help limit distance and reduce ground play, making it a strong choice for precision forehands, controlled backhands, and approaches that need to land soft and stay close to the target.

name: Sol
description:
The Discraft Sol is a 2018 Ace Race Disc. This thin rim, low profile midrange is perfect for touch approach shots.

The Sol is a great disc for players of all skill level, allowing them to shape shots, weave through trees, and hit those difficult lines.

name: Stalker
description: The Discraft Stalker is one of the straightest flying drivers on the market. It offers a narrow rim (1.6cm) that helps provide more control for backhand throws. This disc is perfect for dependable, straight shots and lowering disc golf scores. Discraft gives this disc a stability rating of 1.1.

name: Sting
description: The Discraft Sting was released as the 2017 Ace Race disc. This understable driver provides long glide and more distance for newer disc golfers. The Sting is easy to control and has a Discraft stability rating of 0.0.

name: Surge
description: The Surge is one of Discraft’s signature drivers. This is an outstanding mold, great for intermediate and professional players. The Surge is a fast flying disc with a moderate sized rim. It has a degree of high speed turn, incredible glide, and a healthy amount of low speed fade. The Surge performs well for both backhand and forehand throws. As such a popular disc golf disc, the Surge comes in just about every Discraft plastic. You can buy this disc in low cost Pro-D plastic, mid grade Elite X, durable Z plastic, Premium ESP, and flexible FLX. If you want a unique ultra-colorful Surge, it’s available died in the Z-Plastic.

name: Surge SS
description: The Surge SS is a more understable version of the ultra-popular Discraft Surge. The ‘SS’ has a straighter edge that enhances control-ability and minimizes low speed fade, allowing this disc to fly even farther for less powerful players. The Surge SS works well for both forehand and backhand drives, and is available in ultra durable Elite Z plastic as well as all FLX.

name: Swarm
description: The Discraft Swarm is the newest midrange addition to Discraft's lineup, known for its exceptional stability and reliability. Designed to handle any wind conditions, this disc boasts impressive torque resistance and a dependable fade. Positioned perfectly between the Zone and Captain's Raptor, the Swarm works wonders as a utility disc, ideal for approach shots under 250 feet.7TV6U?//O;Experience the performance of this test flight disc and discover its impressive flight capabilities for yourself.

name: Thrasher
description: The Thrasher is a very fast and understable disc. It is important to know that this mold is not flippy, allowing huge distance from slower arms, and yet is still completely big-arm friendly. It will finish on a slow hyzer in most situations, so expect some clean anhyzers out of this disc. This mold is a great choice for most any skilled player

name: Undertaker
description: The Discraft Undertaker is a moderately overstable driver released in the summer of 2016. This disc offers some turn in flight, but will finish with a manageable fade. The Undertaker will take on headwinds and offer a flight that can be utilized for both backhand and forehand throws.

name: Vulture
description: The Discraft Vulture is described as a beat in Predator that is less overstable and offers more glide. This fairway driver has excellent control and is stable enough to manage moderate headwinds.

name: Wasp
description:
The Wasp is one of the more popular overstable midrange discs. This is a great disc for windy conditions and strategic fading shots. The Wasp has a Discraft stability rating of 1.5. The Wasp is only available in the ultra-durable Elite-Z and ultra-premium Titanium plastics.

If you’re looking for a quality midrange disc that will last, these plastics will do the job. For a more colorful option, consider a dyed Wasp in Elite Z plastic.

name: XL
description: Looking for a straight flying, accurate fairway driver? The Discraft XL is a stable distance driver worth considering. In Pro-D plastic, Discraft gives the XL a stability rating of 0, meaning its overall flight path will be straight. In this plastic, the XL comes in weights as low as 150 grams. In other plastics, this is a slightly overstable disc that will fade more than it will turn (still very minimal). The XL is one of the most popular long range drivers Discraft makes. This disc is used by professionals, but is also a good drivers for beginners because it has so little fade. This disc is available in all the popular plastics Discraft makes: Pro-D, Elite-X, Elite-Z, ESP, and the soft flexible FLX. One interesting thing about the XL is that the stability varies in almost every plastic type. While the stability in Pro D is (0), it is 1 in Elite X, 1.2 in FLX, and 1.5 in ESP and Elite Z.

name: Xtreme
description: The Xtreme is slow but extremely overstable driver. You will use the Discraft Xtreme for utility and distance control shots instead of getting massive distance.

name: Zeus
description: This Paul McBeth signature disc is similar to the popular Innova Destroyer in flight and feel. The Zeus is a go-to driver for power throwers looking for max distance and consistency. The Zeus has a fairly wide rim with a 2.3cm wing length. This is an excellent disc for both forehand and backhand throws for players who can handle the stability. The Zeus is also currently available in Discraft's Swirly ESP plastic.

name: Zombee
description: The Zombee is a fairway driver that was introduced by Discraft for the 2012 Ace Race. This is a tall, super-straight flyer with a predictable end of flight fade. This disc is like a cross between the midrange Buzzz and the Stalker driver. Discraft gives the Zombee a stability rating of 1.0.

name: Zone
description: Want to get in the Zone? The Discraft Zone is a slim putter with a thick rim (1.2cm) that gives you something to hold on to. This is a very overstable putter that is good for headwinds, strategic fades, forehand throws, flick putts, and slamming into chains at just the right angle. Many Discraft enthusiasts will have a Zone in their bag as a putter that gets them out of a situation where a flick, or sidearm putt or approach shot is their only option. Being so overstable, this disc can handle the torque that a flick will put on it! The Zone is considered one of the best Discraft Discs available today.

name: Zone OS
description:
The Zone OS from Discraft is the ultimate utility disc for all disc golfers' on-course needs! This overstable disc has been in high demand for years and can take players' games to the next level.

The Zone OS has earned its stripes as a PDGA certified beefcake, thanks to its impressive torque resistance and assertive fade. Whether a player needs a disc for forehand throws, spike hyzers, or approach shots, the Zone OS can handle it all, even in the toughest of winds and terrain. Its overstable flight makes it perfect for players who demand reliability and consistency from their disc golf equipment. With the Zone OS in their bag, players can take on any challenge with confidence and precision. Try the Zone OS today and take your game to the next level with this versatile and dependable utility disc.

name: Zone SS
description: The Discraft Zone SS is a slightly less overstable version of the classic Zone, offering a straighter flight with minimal fade. It’s designed for controlled approaches, tunnel shots, and straight drives where precision matters, while still maintaining enough stability to finish reliably. Players get the familiar Zone feel but with a smoother, more workable flight right out of the box.

name: Nightjar
description: The Nightjar is a stable, moderate-speed distance driver that delivers long, accurate flights with dependable control. Designed to hold its line even in challenging wind conditions, the Nightjar combines solid glide with manageable speed—making it a great option for players who want reliable distance without overpowering their throw.

name: Skylark
description: The Skylark is a moderately overstable, wide-diameter mid-range designed to help you approach the basket with confidence.

name: Sparrow
description: The Sparrow is a slightly overstable putt-and-approach disc for straight drives, steady approaches, or for your final shots into the chains.

name: Starling
description: The Starling is a straight-flying distance driver. It features a wide rim, is generally lightweight, and is streamlined for long shots without too much end fade.

name: Stork
description: The Stork is a trusty fairway driver that can become a workhorse in your bag to handle your medium to long drives down the fairway. With only slight end-fade, it is a great choice for players at all levels who desire a disc that doesn't exaggerate the side-to-side movement during flight.

name: Woodpecker
description: The Disctroyer Woodpecker looks to be similar to a Zone based on the pictures on the PDGA website. We don't know much about it yet, but we will soon when it releases. It was approved on April 17, 2023

name: Alpas
description: The Alpas, by Divergent Discs, is a very unique putt and approach disc made in flexible silicone rubber material that Divergent calls StayPut. The disc will more successfully "stay put" when it hits the ground, reducing unfortunate skips and slides. It can be used as an understable approach disc to get through tight fairways without needing to throw hard. Or, it can be used as a putter for players who like a shallow rim and a flat disc profile. It is a great choice as an all-around starter disc for new players, as well as a specialty disc for experienced players.

name: Basilisk
description:
The Basilisk, by Divergent Discs, is designed to be the first distance driver for beginners. This has an understable release that gently fades back at the end of its flight and is designed for players with a max distance of 200 feet looking to get more.

The Basilisk has a domed profile and flies with exceptional glide. It's a fun, wide-rimmed driver option that doesn't take a high-speed arm to achieve great distance results. Add some extra feet to your driving game with the Basilisk.

name: Golem
description: The Golem is an overstable putt & approach disc. It has a flat top and a comfortable grip for both backhand and forehand throws. As an overstable putter it will provide a straight flight with no turn and a consistent fade. The Golem can also handle most windy conditions without drastically changing its flight path. It is released in unique Stayput silicone rubber so that it avoids most skips and slides once it lands, plus the material provides a very good grip.

name: Kapre
description: The Kapre is the ideal straight-flying midrange with a lot of glide and minimal side-to-side movement. It can be used with great accuracy on the fairway and will stay under control for tunnel shots and narrow flight paths.

name: Kataw
description: The Divergent Discs Kataw is an overstable midrange disc, expertly designed to offer players enhanced control in windy conditions with consistent performance. This midrange disc glides impressively well for as much stability as it has while ensuring it doesn't turn over, delivering a predictably gradual fading finish on every flight. Featuring a comfortable grip with its flat top and smooth rounded edges, the Kataw is a dependable choice for players across all skill levels, providing reliable performance time and again.

name: Kraken
description: The Kraken is an all-purpose fairway driver that flies with precision to the target. It doesn't have too much fade, nor is it too "flippy" for most players. When thrown with control and a smooth release, the Kraken can fly straight and predictably even for new disc golfers.

name: Lawin
description: Lawin means "Eagle" and this high speed driver is designed to help recreational disc golfers soar past the 300 foot distance barrier. The Lawin can achieve a full S-Curve flight path with moderate power. If the Basilisk becomes too flippy, then the Lawin is the next disc to add to the bag. With its domey profile it also provides excellent glide to carry a few extra feet at the end of its flight.

name: Leviathan
description: The Leviathan is an easy-to-throw midrange disc that works perfectly for new players as a go-to option for minimal fade. It is a very understable, slow-speed disc which means that it is very fade-resistant. If a new disc golfer wants only one disc to get them started, the Leviathan is a great choice. This is one of the best disc golf discs for beginners.

name: Minotaur
description: The Minotaur is an overstable fairway driver that is designed to be a favorite for forehand throws, big hooking hyzers, and shot-shaping flex shots. You can count on the Minotaur when you need a spike hyzer that doesn't go too far, or to go around pesky obstacles.

name: Narwhal
description: The Narwhal is a neutral putter for straight, short-distance flights with a comfortable, beadless rim design. It can be used for short approach shots or for putting into the basket.

name: Nuno
description: The Nuno is an excellent, professional-level putter with a semi-deep rim and no bead. It works great for putting at the basket, or for throwing straight, controlled approach shots.

name: Tiyanak
description: The Tiyanak is an ultra-understable fairway driver designed for the slowest arm speeds. This driver is designed for disc golfers who have a maximum throwing distance of less than 200 feet. New players will find the Tiyanak is one of the easiest drivers to throw because of its high speed turn and resistance to early fade. Young players will also be able to handle this disc with surprising ease. Divergent Discs wanted a disc that anybody could feel comfortable throwing in their very first rounds. More experienced players can find use for the Tiyanak in the understable utility slot of their bag.

name: Abomination
description: The Abomination by Doomsday Discs is a unique disc featuring a variable rim width that transitions from a 7-speed to a 12-speed width. This allows you to select a rim size that feels most comfortable in your grip. The varying rim thickness can affect the disc's spin while in flight, resulting in a distinctive and dynamic flight performance. As the disasterous events of near future cause civilizations to collapse and species to mutate, it is only fitting that the discs of the future should likewise evolve into different shapes and characteristics.

name: Apocalypse
description: There's not a lot of good ways to describe a disc as massively overstable as the Apocalypse by Doomsday Discs because it reaches new levels of ridiculous. It is an ultra-flat-top, high-speed (wide rim) driver, but with so much fade that it immediately dumps, making it almost a "non-distance" driver. It is 100% a utility driver for when players need a disc to instantly fade, or for overhand throws, or playing into headwinds that are strong enough to blow away small livestock. Watch for this disc to become a conversation piece in videos and reviews, and possibly even a tool for throwing contests throughout the disc golf market.

name: Area 51
description: The Area 51 by Doomsday Discs is a stable approach disc that is easy to control whether throwing backhand or forehand. As part of the Conspiracy Line of discs it attests to the fact that there is a lot we are not meant to know, but with the Area 51, you may discover the secret to a better short distance game. Approved on 8/7/23

name: Blackout
description: The Blackout is a control diver that sheds the heavy weight of oppressive governmental regimes in favor of the survivalist instincts of an apocalyptic future. It is also a straight-to-understable disc golf driver that can be manipulated onto any flight path to accurately place shots along the fairway. It is a favorite for controlled, hyzer-flip drives through narrow fairways that are lined by trees, or narrow streets that are lined by abandoned buildings on dystopian disc golf courses. Whether a seasoned disc golfer or a beginner, the Blackout will become a favorite in your bugout disc bag.

name: Bleak
description: The Bleak, by Doomsday Discs, is a top-notch, high-quality, straight-flying putter that perfectly describes the look of the future. While the future may look bleak, your putting doesn't have to if you use one of the best beadless putters on the market. It feels great, looks great, and flies great, whether putting at the basket or tossing the Bleak across the fairway.

name: Bunker Buster
description: The Bunker Buster is an oversized 7-speed with the same diameter as a Condor or Zephyr and comes in weights ranging from 190-200 Grams. This disc is approved by the PDGA as of 7/31/23. This retooled disc is now more overstable than the prototype. It flies straight with insane glide for most of the flight before presenting a prounced end-fade. It can handle power and is extremely dependable for controlled thows across the fairway.

name: Cataclysm
description: The Cataclysm, by Doomsday Discs, is inevitable. Something big is coming and there's no stopping it. But in the meantime, this is a slightly overstable driver that can be handled by players at all levels. It has a comfortable rim configuration for easy release and control.

name: Crisis
description: The Doomsday Crisis is an overstable approach disc meant to save you from the next crisis on the course. This disc will always fade, letting you confidently dodge around gnarled trees, play amist the apocalyptic hurricane, and land next to the basket. This disc is similar in hand feel and flight to the Prodigy A2 or Kastaplast Jarn, but comes in Doomsday's flavorful and distinct plastic blends.

name: Cyber Putter
description: The Cyber Putter by Doosmday Discs was designed as a tribute to the Cyber Truck and is just as edgy and ugly. It has a boxy appearance with sharp edges rather than smooth curves. It is stable enough to handle some torque when thrown and works nicely as a beaded putter to finish at the basket. The sharp edges will wear down quickly after colliding with the chains when used in a softer, putter plastic blend like Ration, but the performance will stay solid.

name: Depth Charge
description: The Depth Charge is an altered version of the original Land Mine, with a narrowed rim to keep it from being too heavy for its size in premium plastic. It has almost identical flight characteristics to the Land Mine, but with even more torque resistance. The Collapse plastic is very flexible and durable, making it useful for sticking where it lands in rugged conditions.

name: Desolation
description: The Desolation, from Doomsday Discs, is an all-purpose, overstable midrange. It has a straight flight that finishes with a moderate, consistent fade. It has smoothed-rim edges and a semi-deep rim to make it very comfortable for throwing and a go-to for seasoned players. It is part of the Catastrophic Line, and made in the USA for high performance that will last to the end of the world (which may come sooner rather than later).

name: Despair
description: The Despair by Doomsday Discs is an all purpose mid-range, designed for straight flight and shot shaping for short distance accuracy. Life may have its fair share of despair, but your disc golf game doesn't need to with a bag of quality discs like this one.

name: Famine
description:
The Doomsday Discs Famine is now PDGA approved and will spread to the masses as an understable-to-neutral distance driver that can be handled by preppers and survivors alike. Whether you're new to the game, or experienced in life's darkest moments, the Famine is inevitable. Whether flexing to great distances or using it to transition into deeper fairways, this disc will find its way into your bag.

This disc is now PDGA approved as of 8/7/23!!

name: Flat Earth
description: As part of the Doomsday Discs "Conspiracy Line", the Flat Earth is a straight-to-understable midrange. It has a micro-bead on the bottom of the rim and is a great choice for narrow or tunnel fairways.

name: Frag
description: The Doomsday Discs Frag may be one of the most overstable discs on the market. This may not be the most versatile disc, but the Frag will consistently fade hard, making it the perfect utility disc.

name: Gloom
description: The Gloom is a beadless putter with a slightly understable flight to resist premature fade when putting at the basket. It has a rim that is slightly altered from the popular Bleak putter from Doomsday, with a less rounded shoulder, a slightly shallower feel, and a more convex wing. This disc was PDGA approved on August 28, 2023.

name: Ice Age
description: The Ice Age by Doomsday Discs is an overstable control driver that can handle as much power as you want to give it without turning over. It will always finish with a noticeable fade, helpful for navigating around obstacles on the course. It's an excellent disc for forehand throwers with a flat top and deep enough rim to get some good "snap" on the release. The Ice Age returns soon, so be ready with an overstable fairway driver in your bag!

name: Land Mine
description: The Land Mine, by Doomsday Discs, is part of the Munitions Line of experimental, unique discs. The Land Mine is an approach putter that has a unique shape with a boxy, blunt leading edge on a thick rim that is wider than most putters. The shape helps to slow down the disc's flight and makes it easy to control with both forehand and backhand throws. This is an ideal approach putter that holds its line and finishes by dropping downward without fading too dramatically. This disc allows you to park it near your desired target without veering off course.

name: Lockdown
description: The Doomsday Discs Lockdown is here. No other distance driver understands the restrictive nature of mandos better than the Lockdown. Its straight flight followed by a steady, reasonable fade will help keep you on the fairway, whether you are supposed to be playing outside or not. This disc has a comfortable, shallow rim perfect for forehand or backhand.

name: Monstrosity
description: The Doomsday Discs Monstrosity is an understable driver with a unique variable rim-width design. Two sides have wide rims and two have narrow rims, giving players different grip options and creating an uneven weight distribution. This affects how the disc spins and flies, making it a fun and unusual option for testing new lines, release angles, or throwing styles. It’s a true experiment in disc design. The Monstrosity IS PDGA Approved.

name: Oblivion
description: The Oblivion by Doomsday Discs is a pro-level, 12-speed distance driver designed to handle high-power throws for maximum distance performance. Experienced players should be able to throw this driver for serious gains and still watch it predictably fade into the oblivion.

name: Pestilence
description: The Pestilence, by Doomsday Discs, is part of the Catastrophic Line of discs. It is a wide-rim distance driver that is understable enough to be handled by new players. For experienced players, it is an excellent tool for hyzer-flip and long s-curve shots. Reach your maximum potential on long throws without needing maximum arm speed or power. After the world comes crumbling down, you'll be able to use the Pestilence well into the dystopian future.

name: Plague
description: The Doomsday Discs Plague is a stable distance driver for survivors of the doomsday. It will spread infectiously through ruined civilizations and dystopian landscapes for many decades and centuries to come.

name: Proximity Mine
description: The Proximity Mine is another approach putter in the “Land Mine” family. It’s a large, boxy putter but with a more overstable, angled rim and a thumb track on the top of the flight plate. It is a stable, slow-speed approach disc that is great for short throws where accuracy is important and overthrowing the target is unwanted.

name: Psyop
description: The Psyop is an understable 9-speed, fast enough for positive distance, but slow enough to be easily caught in the psychological operations that sway the masses into compliance. The Psyop can be handled by slower arm speeds as well as by experienced players who need an anhyzer flight path or a flip-up disc in controlled circumstances. Every throw is a test of compliance. Believe what you’re told, or learn what the Psyop can really do.

name: Rot
description: The Rot by Doomsday Discs is a beaded putter. This disc feels amazing in the hand, with a smooth, rounded rim and a medium, perfect sized bead. This disc is flat topped for a comfortable and consistent delivery. The Rot boasts the perfect stability and glide to be able to be your go-to putting putter. It's also torque-resistant enough to be amazing off the tee.

name: Scavenger
description: The Scavenger by Doomsday Discs is an easy-to-throw, understable mid-range with a slightly more shallow profile than the Despair. It's a good choice for players who prefer gripping a low-profile midrange. It was officially approved by the PDGA on 8/7/23.

name: Scope
description: The Scope is a necessary tool to zoom-in and isolate your target before taking your shot, then release it and watch it hit the mark. This approach midrange from Doomsday Discs has a unique rim configuration that may take you by surprise at first, but after one throw and you'll find that the grip is comfortable, the release predictable, and the flight very accurate with torque resistance to hold the line.

name: Solar Death Ray
description: The Solar Death Ray by Doomsday Discs is a one-of-a-kind fairway driver that features flight rings on the top of the flight plate. Those grooves provide an extra grip and unique texture when holding and releasing the disc. It has a neutral flight that will take the path upon which the thrower releases it, providing some understable turn before a nearly equal end-fade. Though the Solar Death Ray will do your bidding on the disc golf course, it may likely signal the destruction of modern civilization as we know it.

name: Wasteland
description: In any apocalyptic situation, you can always expect to find some barren Wasteland. That's why it's perfect to have a slightly overstable go-to midrange in your Doomsday Discs lineup. This disc has a shallow profile and a comfortable, smooth rim shape.

name: WMD
description: The WMD is a "Weapon of Maximum Distance" from Doomsday Discs. It is NOT approved by the PDGA because it is heavier than would be approved for a driver of its size, plus it has a very wide rim to make it even more "high speed". Despite the wide rim and weight, it has an understable turn so that you can hyzer-flip it up and get a giant s-curve for maximum distance. It's a lot of fun to throw and can be used in your recreational rounds. Just don't pull this thing out in a PDGA sanctioned tournament unless you plan on wearing sunglasses and dark clothing as you stealthily tuck it back into your bag before anybody can see what you've unleashed on the course.

name: Arowana
description: Approved on Nov 7, 2022 this Elevation putter will fly straight as an arrow for you.  It's an awesome approach putter for any skill level.  This will initially be released in ecoFLEX plastic which makes it firm enough to be a solid throwing putter, but still with enough bend to reduce unwanted ground play.

name: Binx
description:
The Elevation Disc Golf Binx is a straight-flying fairway driver that is useful for everyone. Its flight characteristics allow for easy control and consistent flight paths. The Binx can hold most any line you need, proving to be a good go-to driver.

The unique rubber blend is designed to limit ground play when the disc lands. This ensures accuracy and consistency on those upshots that have OB or other hazards around the landing zone.

name: Capybara
description: The Elevation Capybara is an understable midrange with a low profile and a smooth, comfortable rim. It’s easy to control and great for players looking to shape lines with finesse. With just a touch of turn, the Capybara excels at hyzer flips, turnovers, and straight shots that carry farther than expected. It’s a reliable option for slower arm speeds or for players who want glide and control without added effort.

name: Gecko
description: The Gecko is the ultimate addition to the Elevation Disc Golf lineup, fulfilling a longstanding need for a reliable and dependable disc. Designed by mold designer and Elevation co-founder Austin Minnocci, this disc offers unparalleled performance on the course. Thanks to the ecoFLEX material, the Gecko is able to handle the full power of even the most skilled players without any hesitation, while still maintaining flexibility for incredible accuracy and control. Say goodbye to bad groundplay and hello to dependable flights with the Gecko.

name: Interceptor
description:
The Interceptor is a stable to overstable approach mid. It's made out of a soft, durable rubber. It was designed to be the perfect approach & layup disc that will stick exactly where it lands! The Interceptor has more weight around the rim and a thinner flight plate, giving you great grip on a soft rubber disc. It has a small groove on the flight plate, creating the perfect grip for forehand or backhand throws.

Stability varies greatly from run to run as Elevation uses a variety of recylced materials.

name: Koi
description: The Koi is designed to glide to the basket with ease. This straight-to-understable putter will hold whatever line you put it on, and Elevation's floppy rubber material will ensure it sticks and grabs onto the chains. It was approved by the PDGA on May, 2021

name: Nimbus
description: The Elevation Discs Nimbus is a neutral midrange disc currently available in the ecoFLEX recycled rubber blend. It will hold whatever line is put on it, both forehand and backhand. As a midrange that doesn’t flip up or have an aggressive fade, the Nimbus will be a staple in every disc golfer’s bag.

name: Psychic
description:
Introducing the Psychic, Elevation Disc Golf's first-ever distance driver! Designed for exceptional performance, this low-profile disc offers a comfortable grip and flies straight as an arrow. Crafted from ecoFLEX material for superior grip even in bad weather, the Psychic's innovative design delivers enhanced stability for impressive distance shots. If you're looking for a disc that bombs and stops with precision, the Psychic is your go-to choice.

With a flight plate 35% thinner than traditional injection molding processes and rubber materials 30% denser than plastic, the "Psychic" by Elevation Disc Golf promises unmatched stability and impressive performance on the course.

name: Screech
description: The Screech by Elevation Disc Golf is a dependable overstable approach disc designed for both backhand and forehand throws. With a touch more stability and lower glide than traditional approach discs, the Screech hooks up at the end of its flight, providing precise control. Made with Elevation’s signature rubber material, it’s built to stick where it lands, giving you added confidence and consistency on every throw.

name: Honey
description: The Essential Discs Honey stands out as an overstable approach disc with a sleek, low-profile design. Engineered for swift hand releases and minimal glide, it delivers exceptional precision on every throw. Its distinctive weight distribution, focused primarily on the slightly oversized rim, uniquely positions the Honey at the intersection of midrange and putter capabilities, offering players a versatile and innovative option for their game.

name: Oxygen
description: The Essential Discs Oxygen, an understable distance driver, excels in executing hyzer flips and rollers, making it an ideal choice for players with moderate arm speeds. Its versatility shines, catering to disc golfers across all skill levels with its user-friendly performance.

name: Rain
description: The Rain from Essential Discs is an overstable fairway driver built for control and reliable fade. It handles power and wind with ease, making it a solid choice for skipping around corners or shaping shots that need a firm finish. The Rain delivers consistent performance for players who want a dependable fairway that holds up in tough conditions.

name: Delivery
description: The Delivery is an excellent putter when you just want to deliver the final toss into the basket. It's a straight-flying, beaded putter for comfortable handling and easy control.

name: Transition
description: The Transition by Eurodisc is a quintisential mid-range for shorter, precision throws that have minimal movement from side-to-side. Transition from the tee to the basket with this top-notch midrange.

name: Transporter
description: The Transporter by Eurodisc is an understable fairway driver with high glide, designed to be a go-to for controlled placement and good distance even for newer players. Transport your way accross the fairway with a disc that is easy and fun to throw.

name: Agera
description: The EV-7 Agera is a versatile putt and approach disc with a shallower rim and a beveled edge closer to a midrange than a traditional putter. This design creates a controllable, understable flight that works well for drives and approaches while remaining smooth and straight on the green.

name: Mobius
description: EV-7 claims the Möbius has done the "impossible" by creating the straightest putter ever made. It has the perfect combination of smooth turn and torque resistance. Players who use the Möbius will have the ability to hit laser straight lines and very long putts with accuracy and full confidence. The Mobius is one of our highest rated straight flying understable putters.

name: Penrose
description: This is an overstable putt and approach disc, designed to be consistent and reliable. You can easily throw this disc and watch it glide to the target.

name: Phi
description: The Phi is a beadless putter that has less fade than the Penrose, providing a stable enough putter to handle headwinds and handle those approach shots with excellent glide. This makes for a great driving putter and a dependable putting putter.

name: Telos
description:
The EV-7 Telos is a fantastic putter that fits right in between the Penrose and the Phi in terms of stability. This disc has the flat top like the Penrose, but the beadless bottom like the Phi. This is an excellent stability for an all purpose disc golf putter and will help you find the green and make more putts. 

The Telos is available in all of the outstanding EV-7 premium putter blends.

name: Yang
description: The EV-7 Yang is a slow, slightly overstable approach disc, designed as the counterpart to the Yin. Inspired by the Golden Ratio, the Yang features a concave wing radius and a big bead, creating a disc that excels at ground play. Its shallow profile and reduced glide help keep shots within 250 feet, allowing it to skip on hyzer lines and softly slide on straight approaches. With its reliable flatten and fade on anhyzers, the Yang provides controlled, consistent finishes for players of all levels.

name: Yin
description: The EV-7 Yin is an air shot approach disc inspired by the concept of balance and harmony with its counterpart, the Yang. Featuring a design rooted in the Golden Ratio, the Yin has a convex wing radius, creating a neutral, beadless disc that holds any line without harsh fade. This slow and shallow disc glides smoothly toward the green, making it ideal for controlled shots within 250 feet. With its gentle finish and consistent flight, the Yin offers players a balanced and reliable option for precise approaches.

name: Havu
description: The HAVU bridges the gap between a putter and a midrange disc, offering a straight flight path that is easy to control and maintains its angle consistently. This disc is remarkably versatile, catering to players of all skill levels, from novices to professionals. With a strong throw, the HAVU can be induced to turn over towards the end of its flight, demonstrating its adaptable flight characteristics. The name "Havu" refers to a fresh sprig or small branch from a coniferous tree, embodying the lush, green forests that define Finland's breathtaking landscapes, where coniferous trees are a significant and beautiful part of the natural environment.

name: Kanto
description: Exel's big bead mid-range disc, known as the Kanto, combines straight flight characteristics with a gentle end fade, ensuring it maintains the desired throwing angle and boasts excellent aerodynamic properties. This disc is engineered to endure forceful throws while remaining accessible to beginners, striking a perfect balance between durability and user-friendliness. The term "Kanto," in Finnish, means "stump" or "tree stump," referring to the residual portion of a tree's trunk and its root system left in the ground after the tree has been felled or has fallen naturally, symbolizing both stability and resilience.

name: Kelo
description: The Exel Discs' Kelo is an overstable fairway driver designed for powerful throwers. Whether facing headwinds or tailwinds, Kelo remains consistently reliable even in challenging wind conditions. It exhibits less glide and a strong fade, ensuring dependable performance in strong winds.

name: Korpi
description: The Korpi is an understable distance driver renowned for its exceptional glide. When thrown with force, it demonstrates a notable high-speed turn followed by a lengthy, forward-pushing fade. At lower speeds, the disc behaves more like a stable disc, making the Korpi an easily manageable high-speed driver. It is versatile, suitable for both backhand and forehand throws. The name "Korpi" in Finnish signifies a type of natural landscape or wilderness area, often a remote, wild, and largely unspoiled forest region, characterized by its rugged beauty, dense forests, numerous lakes, and pristine natural ecosystems, far from human development.

name: Nila
description:
The NILA stands out as an overstable putt and approach disc, distinguished by its exceptional high-speed torque resistance. This feature ensures reliable performance and trustworthiness in windy conditions, making it an ideal choice for players with powerful throws or those who require consistent disc fading under any circumstances.

The name "Nila" has its roots in Finnish, where it translates to "phloem" in English. Phloem is an essential plant tissue that plays a vital role in transporting nutrients—most notably, sugars generated through photosynthesis—from the leaves to other parts of the plant, including the roots and stems. This process is pivotal in distributing organic compounds across the plant, promoting growth, and sustaining the health of various plant tissues.

name: Puro
description: The Exel Puro is a versatile understable fairway driver suitable for a wide range of players. For professionals, it excels in executing long, gliding turnover shots, whereas beginners and amateurs will find it perfect for achieving straight shots with ease. Characterized by its low fade and impressive glide, the Puro is an excellent choice for anyone's first driver. The term "Puro," in Finnish, means "stream" or "brook," referring to a small, often picturesque watercourse flowing through varied landscapes like forests, meadows, or hills, symbolizing the Puro's smooth and natural flight path through the air.

name: Tuohi
description: The Tuohi is a premium overstable midrange disc designed for players seeking consistent performance under all wind conditions. Featuring a pronounced fade and exceptional torque resistance, the Tuohi excels in navigating around obstacles or trees, making it an indispensable choice for challenging courses. The name "Tuohi," translating to "birch bark" in English, is inspired by the protective outer layer of the birch tree (genus Betula), a material revered for its durability and versatility across various cultures throughout history. This disc embodies the resilience and natural elegance of its namesake, offering players a reliable and high-quality option for their disc golf arsenal.

name: Chicane
description: The Chicane by Finish Line is an overstable approach putter that can handle confident backhand and forehand throws. It has a flat top and a comfortable rim for easy grip and will hold the line on any approach shot with a solid fade at the end of flight. The Chicane is a valuable tool on the fairway and will help you park your shot for a solid finish at the basket.

name: Daytona
description: The Daytona represents the pinnacle of overstable distance drivers, offering unwavering consistency and a comfortable grip. This disc will handle windy conditions, maintaining its consistent overstable flight path with each throw. It's the perfect choice for executing flex shots and overhand throws with dependability.

name: Era
description:
The Era is the first disc in Drew Gibson's Finish Line. This disc is designed to be the all-purpose control driver for players of all skill levels. 

With a flat top and neutral flight path the Era works well for both backhand and forehand throws. It is understable enough to work for newer players, but has enough stability that it can handle the power of Drew Gibson. The Era has enough speed and glide to go as far as many distance drivers, but has minimal fade for the straight flight of a fairway.

Finish Line Discs are designed and manufactured in the USA.

name: Interval
description: The Interval by Finish Line is an exciting distance driver for all intermediate to advanced players.This 12-speed powerhouse propels your game like a high-performance racing car, hurtling towards extraordinary distances with the precision of a finely-tuned machine. With minimal turn and a slight end-fade, the Interval is not too hard to handle, so buckle up for an exhilarating ride as you attack the course with the Interval.

name: Pace
description:
The Pace is the first putt and approach disc in Drew Gibson's Finish Line.

This tall beadless putter features a mostly flat top and stable flight. It has enough stability to handle Drew Gibson's power, but works great for putting and throwing for disc golfers of all skill levels.

name: Rally
description:
The Rally from Finish Line Discs is amazing. It's a *straight flying, glidey fairway like no other. It's workable on any line, and just goes where you want it to go. While it's straight flying, it's surprisingly torque resistant, and can hold up to lots of power. 

Get ready for the Rally.  

*Note. The Launch Edition and First Run Rally's have a little more dome and more over-stability than the prototype and first run discs with flight ratings of 8/5/0/2. First run Rally's have a bit more high speed turn and an overall straighter flight path with flight ratings 8/5/-1/2.

name: Sector
description: The Sector by Finish Line is a stable midrange disc with only moderate end-fade, designed to be handled expertly by professional players as well as newer players. It is a precision tool in the bag for shorter drives, narrow fairways, and low cielings. It's a slightly more overstable midrange option than the Finish Line Supra.

name: Supra
description: The Finish Line Supra is the do-it-all midrange designed by Drew Gibson who is known as one of the best midrange throwers in all of disc golf. This workhorse of a midrange is highly capable, and easily holds many different lines, providing a manageable disc for all players regardless of skill. The Supra is a formidable disc that will easily be a go-to disc.

name: Torque
description:
The Torque, from Finish Line, is an overstable, trusty fairway driver. It isn't quite Firebird levels of overstable but it will hold up to as much power as you can give it and will come back for a nice flex line.

PDGA Approved on 7/30/23.

name: Dot
description: The Dot by Flight Lab Discs is a straight-flying, laser accurate putter crafted for players who crave consistency on the green. This putter delivers a buttery-smooth grip, perfect for confident releases and pinpoint control. Whether you're sinking short putts or floating dead-straight approaches, The Dot holds the line with no fade and not fuss-- just clean, dependable flight.

name: Knockout
description: The Knockout by Flight Lab Discs is a rounded-shoulder, flat-topped, micro-beaded 9-speed with a uniquely beefy feel, built for serious stability. It is an aggressive flyer with high torque resistance for headwind control, high-skipping hyzers, and force-flex lines that finish on command. It also features a shallow bead on the inside of the rim-- unique to drivers.

name: Vapor
description: The Vapor by Flight Lab is a stable-overstable powerhouse disc built for controlled distance. Designed for confident throws off the tee, it handles torque beautifully and finishes with a predictable, late fade—no flutter, no doubt.

name: Wayfinder
description: The Wayfinder by Flight Lab Discs is a reinvented mid-range disc designed for players who demand consistency and control. With a balanced flight profile and a comfortable grip, it has a nice consistent finish at the end of its flight.

name: Beacon
description: The Beacon is an overstable midrange disc with a very predictable flight. It is great for backhands and forehands and is used for upshots or off the tee.

name: Cruise
description: The Cruise is Full Turn's newest and fastest disc, coming in at speed 13. However, it is manageable by all skill levels with early turn in the flight, and late fade with great glide.

name: Manifest
description:
The Manifest is an overstable putter that features a thumbtrack for increased grip. The thumbtack is in a comfortable place regardless of whether you use a fan grip or a power grip. 

The overstability makes this a good putter to use for drives or for those powerful spin putts. This disc flies straight with a reliable fade at the end.

name: Passport
description: The Passport is the document you must present to reach your final destination, and this introductory putter from Full Turn Discs is your passport to the basket as you complete each hole.

name: Shortcut
description: The Shortcut is a very overstable approach disc. The stability makes this an excellent utility disc. It will not glide very far, and it fades hard, allowing you to get around a tight corner, or out of some tough situation.

name: Orbit
description: The Orbit is a moderately overstable midrange. This design is suitable for even strong throwers. The Orbit will fly in any wind conditions. You can count on this disc.

name: Shooting Star
description: The Shooting star is a fairway driver with a manageable speed rating. The unique low-profile design cuts through the wind with a steady, gentle flip. This is a great disc for beginners and throwers with low-to-medium power. It can be used by power throwers for long rollers as well. The Shooting Star is a slightly understable driver.

name: Ambush
description: The Goliath Discs Ambush is a high-speed, understable distance driver. It's perect for newer players who are trying to gain new distance, or for more advanced players who want amazing rollers or huge understable bombs.

name: Ark
description: The Goliath Discs Ark is a stable 9-speed fairway driver that slots in between an Enoch and a Chainbreaker. Not being too overstable or understable, the ark will become your reliable 9-speed driver!

name: Chainbreaker
description: The Goliath Discs Chainbreaker, their premier OVERSTABLE fairway driver, conquers even the fiercest winds, and in the right hands, it may just fulfill its moniker.

name: Covenant
description: The Covenant (formarly the Genesis) is a beadless putter with nice glide and a neutral flight. This offers excellent control for those with touch. This disc is capable of flying perfectly straight, holding any anhyzer lines, being a great hyzer flip disc, as well as being your go-to putter.

name: Enoch
description: The Goliath Discs Enoch is an impressive fairway driver with a speed rating of 8, boasting a remarkable glide of 5, a neutral turn of 0, and a reliable fade of 1.5. Designed to handle substantial power, the Enoch delivers consistent and dependable flight characteristics. Whether you're seeking accuracy, distance, or control, the Enoch is the go-to choice for disc golfers looking for reliability and performance on the course.

name: Jireh
description: The Jireh is the first Goliath Discs mold. This midrange is a high-glide neutral 5-speed capable of handling various lines and to push forward before fading. With easy to throw tendencies, this midrange is a controllable and accurate disc proving to have the means to be your do-it-all midrange. Overall, the Goliath has the ability to fit into anyone's bag.

name: Pillar
description: The Goliath Discs Pillar is a very overstable putt & approach disc. It will guide you to the basket with unwavering overstable presicion.

name: Rapture
description: The Rapture is a long-bomb, overstable distance driver from Goliath Discs. Advanced players will find the Rapture to make their distance shots more reliable!

name: Redemption
description: Meet the Goliath Discs Redemption, the bigger sibling of the Jireh. A dependable finish to the left (ideal for right-handed backhand players) makes it an essential addition to your bag.

name: Samson
description: The Goliath Discs Samson is a controllable midrange with a dependable fade, built to handle power while maintaining a reliable finish. Designed for players who need consistency and control, the Samson resists turning under torque and delivers a smooth, predictable flight. Power throwers will appreciate its ability to handle high-speed throws without losing stability, making it a trusted option for both controlled approaches and longer midrange shots.

name: Shepherd
description: The Shepherd appears to be a distance driver by Goliath Discs.

name: Slingshot
description: The Goliath Discs Slingshot is a big distance, 13-speed driver that is not for the feint of heart. The Slingshot is a straight flyer, featuring some turn and some fade. This disc has the potential to take your distance game to the next level.

name: Waymaker
description: The Goliath Discs Waymaker is a beaded, slightly overstable putter designed to offer a perfect balance of control and stability. As the first beaded putter from Goliath Discs, the Waymaker provides a reliable option for precise putting and approach shots, with stability that falls between the Genesis and the Pillar.

name: Cirrus
description:
The Guru Cirrus is a slow-speed midrange with a thin rim, low profile, and flat top. The disc weight is of the Cirrus is much lighter than most disc golf discs.

This midrange disc is very similar to the Cumulus. It is available in ultra-soft, flexible, light-weight plastic and is ideal for children.

name: Contrail
description:
The Guru Contrail is an ultra-light-weight, super-soft fairway driver designed for beginners. 

This easy to throw fairway driver is incredibly flexible and will fold in half. It also floats in water. The Contrail is a great driver to use for children and school group disc golf instruction.

name: Cumulus
description:
The Guru Cumulus is a light-weight, easy-to-throw putter available in an ultra-soft plastic blend. The plastic used is so flexible that you can literally fold the Cumulus in half. This is a beadless putter with a flat top.

This putt and approach disc is perfect for children and others just learning disc golf. 

The Cumulus will float in water.

name: Flow Motion
description:
The Guru Flow Motion is a flat-top, beadless putter with a stable flight path. This putt and approach disc works well for both approach shots and short putts. 

This is an excellent all-purpose putter for players of all skill levels.

name: Mad Mission
description: The Guru Mad Mission is a stable flying approach/midrange with a flat top and thin rim. This disc excels for short drives and consistent approach shots.

name: Night Trooper
description: The Guru Night Trooper is an easy to throw fairway driver designed to give new disc golfers maximum distance and control.

name: Short Slacker
description:
The Guru Short Slacker is a low-profile beginner-friendly distance driver designed to give growing players more distance than the driver found in the starter set. 

When thrown with high speeds the Short Slacker exhibits a significant amount of high speed turn for gliding S-Curve flight paths. New players will find that the Short Slacker has a significant amount of low speed end of flight fade.

name: Silent Cruiser
description: The Guru Silent Cruiser Midrange is an overstable utility disc designed to fade hard. This disc is classified as a midrange but has a rim configuration more similar to a driver.

name: Adventurer
description: The H13 Disc Golf Adventurer is a 7-speed fairway driver designed for versatility and control. Debuting in Journey Bound and Travel Bound premium blends, it delivers smooth 'S' flights with slight turn and fade. Capable of holding an anhyzer line under torque with minimal fade, the Adventurer is a dependable choice for shaping lines and reaching the green with precision.

name: Companion
description: The H13 Disc Golf Companion is a reliable point-and-shoot approach disc. With a speed of 4, it bridges the gap between a putter and a midrange. Designed to complement your game, the Companion is a dependable go-to for approach shots. It handles torque with ease, staying on hyzer with little to no flip-up and finishing with a consistent fade.

name: Embark
description: The H13 Disc Golf Embark is a beaded putter with medium depth and excellent glide. It performs reliably for both Circle 1 and Circle 2 putts. Off the tee or on approach shots, the Embark handles torque well, offering controlled turn for turnovers and flip-ups with a consistent, small fade to finish. A dependable addition to any bag, the Embark is ready for putting and throwing with precision.

name: Dime
description: The Hooligan Discs Dime is a beaded putter with flight numbers 2 / 4 / 0 / 1, offering a straight flight path with a touch of fade at the end. Known for its impressive glide and reliable stability, the Dime covers both putting and approach situations, making it an essential addition to any disc golfer's bag.

name: Thread
description: The Alpha Thread is a straight-to-understable control driver. Beginner players with a slower arm speed may enjoy using this disc to thread lines through a tree filled fairway, backhand or forehand. Intermediate players could use this disc for hyzer flip shots. Advanced players may utilize this disc for rollers to slice through tight lines.

name: Sabot
description: The Sabot is a high speed, overstable driver designed for power throwers. You can put all the muscle you want behind this driver and still get a dependable fade. Use the Sabot to fight into headwinds, to navigate around tight curves, or to throw hyzer bombs over the trees.

name: Alpaca
description: The Alpaca is the quintessential beadless putter and is consistently rated as one of the best putt and approach discs in all of disc golf. The Alpaca has a deep, comfortable, traditional, and familiar feel. The Alpaca is the putting putter of choice for nearly all of Infinite's sponsored pros. This goes to show how incredible this putter really is. It is one of the greats inside the circle, and has incredible glide and torque resistance for as far as you can huck it.

name: Anubis
description: The Anubis is a straight-flying, all-purpose, precision mid-range disc that will fit into the bags of both novice and professional players. Every player can count on the Anubis to hold the line whether thrown for shorter approaches or for longer distances off the tee. The Anubis will help players to fine-tune their throwing skills by flying precisely in accordance with the release from the hand.

name: Artifact
description: The Artifact is an understable, easy-to-control putter with a rounded, beadless rim. It is comfortable to throw for short drives and approaches, holding the line upon which it is released while reducing any end-of-flight fade. It's also a trusty putter to finish at the basket and an excellent choice for players who prefer to use a "loft putt" style or who cherish minimal fade into the chains.

name: Aztec
description:
The Aztec, classified as a speed 10 driver, exhibits a straight flight path with a subtle fade towards the end. Despite its speed rating, the Aztec surprises most players with remarkable distance and accuracy, proving itself as a valuable asset to any game.

Unlike high-speed distance drivers, the Aztec boasts a more slender rim, enhancing comfort for the majority of disc golfers. Widely recognized as a lengthier yet comparable flying disc to the Exodus or Dynasty, the Aztec promises a blend of performance and familiarity for players seeking reliable distance shots.

name: Centurion
description:
The Centurion, a versatile fairway driver, is designed to offer exceptional workability on the disc golf course. Tailored for both novice and professional players, it stands out as a straight-flying, precision driver that seamlessly integrates into any player's bag.

No matter the skill level, every player can rely on the Centurion to maintain the desired line with just a subtle end fade. This workhorse control driver excels in providing precise placement on the fairway, making it an invaluable addition to any disc golfer's arsenal.

Whether you're navigating tricky fairways or seeking consistent, accurate throws, the Centurion rises to the occasion, proving itself as a go-to disc for players aiming for both control and reliability.

name: Chariot
description: The Chariot is a straight-flying midrange disc with excellent glide and a knack for following the thrower's angle of release. It can be guided on smooth hyzer lines, on anhyzer turns, or can be used for straight, precision approaches on the fairway. The Chariot finishes with a gentle and consistent fade and is ready for frequent use in your disc golf game. This disc features a comfortable microbead that adds a touch more torque resistance and durability. All Infinite Discs branded discs are manufactured by Innova Champion Discs.

name: Cohort
description: The Cohort is a shallower compliment to the Alpaca. This disc is a perfect fit for a player with smaller hands or those that prefer a smaller hand-feel for their putter. It's a straight flyer that can handle a decent amount of power and also perform well for touchy upshots. This disc falls somewhere inbetween a putt and approach and a midrange disc, and can be easily used for both. The shallow grip makes this disc comfortable for both backhands and forehands.

name: Conqueror
description: The Conqueror by Infinite Discs is a formidable overstable distance driver designed to conquer the challenges of the most powerful throws, even in the hands of professional players. When unleashed, the Conqueror showcases an impressive end-of-flight fade, whether launched forehand or backhand, and dependably conquers fierce headwinds with unwavering stability.

name: Czar
description:
The Czar is an impressive overstable speed-11 driver that bridges the gap between power and precision, akin to a slower-paced Emperor. Tailored for intermediate and advanced players, it provides heightened control without sacrificing the thrill of a dynamic throw.

Built to resist torque, the Czar empowers players to rip confident shots with ease. Consider it not just as an overstable option but a dependable and consistent alternative to the Wraith, ensuring your throws navigate the course with authority and predictability. Enhance your game with the commanding performance of the Czar!

name: Dynasty
description:
The Dynasty stands out as the ultimate go-to control driver, catering to players of all skill levels. Whether you're a beginner looking to enhance your distance game or an advanced player seeking precision, the Dynasty proves to be the perfect choice. Surprisingly, despite its 9-speed rating, the Dynasty often outperforms traditional distance drivers due to its exceptional glide.

This disc's ability to maintain a straight flight over extended fairways makes it a preferred choice for accurate throws. Take your game to the next level with the Dynasty – the epitome of control and versatility in the disc golf world!

name: Emperor
description:
Master the courses with the Emperor by Infinite Discs. This commanding distance driver, boasting a moderate overstable nature, stands unrivaled among its counterparts in the market. Experience the sheer power as the Emperor effortlessly glides through the air, outclassing competitors and setting new standards for distance drivers.

It's the go-to choice for a multitude of Infinite sponsored players, despite having the freedom to select from any brand. Grab an Emperor today and experience its unparalleled performance firsthand.

name: Exodus
description: The Exodus was the first disc in the Infinite Discs lineup, and it’s a solid, easy-to-control fairway driver. Its comfortable rim and moderate speed make it great for players at any skill level. Known for its dependability, the Exodus shines on wooded courses, offering a precise, long flight with a consistent fade. It handles powerful throws well, making it capable of impressive long-distance shots.

name: Galleon
description: The Infinite Discs Galleon is a disc crafted to redefine your disc golf toolkit. This super-overstable, deliberately slow fairway driver is not just a disc; it's a versatile solution tailored for a variety of specialty shots. Whether you're navigating tight fairways, executing forehand rollers, launching overhead shots, or finding creative solutions for those challenging stuck-behind-something scenarios, the Galleon has got you covered.

name: Glyph
description:
The Glyph is a versatile, slow-speed putter, perfect for precise putting and approaching the basket across the fairway. It's also great for a friendly game of catch, whether you're warming up or just having a casual toss with friends.

The Glyph is also a limited edition disc that will only be released in limited quantities once per year in the Infinite Discs line.

name: Inca
description: The Inca is a workhorse, beaded, torque resistant midrange. This disc will be your go-to for years to come because of its amazing ability to get better over time. It's overstable now, but throw it for a season or two and it will be the straightest flying, most dependable midrange you've ever thrown. This midrange was made to cycle, and was designed for that purpose.

name: Khonsu
description: The Khonsu is an awesome, understable, beadless addition to Infinite Discs's midrange line up. Throw the Khonsu with a hyzer release and it will pop-up to fly extremely straight. Or give it a slight anhyzer release and it will hold the line when trying to get around obstacles in your path.  This disc feels excellent and is a must for anyone needing an understable midrange.

name: Kon Tiki
description: The Kon Tiki is a midrange approach disc designed for straight-to-understable touch shots, approaching the basket, or weaving through tight fairways. It can be easily used to hold anhyzer flights as an option for players who don't feel comfortable with their forehand game. It has a low profile with a small rim depth and a small bead. The dimensions and flight characteristics of the Kon Tiki make it a great choice for beginners and players with slow arm speeds, or as a go-to midrange for finesse shots.

name: Maya
description: The Maya emerges as an exceptional understable distance bomber! Ideal for beginners and intermediate players, this disc proves to be an excellent choice for straight-flying distance drives. Meanwhile, advanced to open players harness the Maya's capabilities for delivering massive understable distance shots with finesse and precision. Discover the versatility and dynamic performance of the Maya across all skill levels.

name: Myth
description: The MYTH is the first putter in the Infinite Discs brand lineup and we wanted to start strong. The Myth is a dependable, beaded putter with a comfortable rim that can be used for precision putting, or as a driving putter and approach disc. If your putting game can't be a thing of legend, then at least let it be a thing of Myth. Inspire friends and family to talk about your epic putts around late-night campfires for generations to come.

name: Pharaoh
description:
The Pharaoh stands as the ultimate distance driver, designed for high-speed releases and maximum distance potential. Its manageable fade sets it apart, ensuring that even intermediate players can harness its wonders. When you need to conquer extensive territory off the tee, trust the Pharaoh to deliver. Rule your local disc golf course with the unmatched power of the Pharaoh!

NOTE: The flight numbers may change on different runs. For example, the "Run 3" I-Blend Pharaoh is more understable and has a flight rating of 13 / 6 / -2 / 2 which is different than the first two runs which were on Swirly S-Blend plastic. The Run 5 Swirly S-Blend Pharaoh is more overstable than any previous run.

name: Ra
description: The Infinite Discs Ra is a moderately overstable midrange with a very flat top and comfortable, beaded rim. It utilizes Innova's V-technology to provide incredible torque resistance. It feels and flies perfectly for both backhand and forehand players. It is difficult to find a midrange that is so easy to control and so precise under multiple course conditions.

name: Raze
description: The Raze seamlessly fills a gap in the Infinite Discs lineup, introducing a forehand-friendly, overstable approach disc distinguished by its flat or puddle top and smooth tactile feel. Tailored for precision, it excels in approach shots, power drives off the tee, and a myriad of other scenarios you can envision on the disc golf course. This versatile disc not only addresses a specific need but also brings a new level of control and performance to your game.

name: Roman
description: The Infinite Discs Roman is a power driver, designed to provide unrivaled control and a dependable trajectory in all weather conditions. It's designed for experienced players, but even beginners can benefit from its dependable overstable flight for windy conditions and flex shots. With the ability to enhance accuracy on both backhand and forehand throws, the Roman is an exceptional choice for any player looking for consistent reliability. The Roman pairs nicely as a more overstable companion to the Dynasty, or a glidier companion to the Scepter.

name: Scarab
description: The Scarab is a beaded putter that has a similar rim configuration to the Infinite Discs Myth, but with a different flight plate that provides a longer glide for longer putts and approaches. If you love throwing putters for distance, or you're seeking pinpoint accuracy with your putting game, the Scarab will earn a spot in your bag.

name: Scepter
description:
The Scepter, a flat-top overstable fairway driver, is crafted for power throwers or those seeking utility on the disc golf course.. Designed with a flat top, it offers exceptional comfort for forehand use and reliably fights out of anhyzer releases.

Count on the Scepter to seamlessly slice through headwinds, showcasing its resilience and stability even in challenging weather conditions. This dynamic disc ensures a dramatic end-of-flight fade, adding precision to your throws and making it an ideal choice for players who prioritize control and reliability. Dominate the course with the Scepter in your arsenal!

name: Slab
description: The Slab, characterized by its flat top and wide rim, is a high-speed, extremely overstable distance driver. Tailored for power throwers, it ensures you a reliable fade even under intense force. This versatile disc excels in various scenarios, from executing spike hyzer throws and powerful forehands to battling the wind and serving as a utility disc for navigating tight fades around obstacles. Watch the Slab slice dependably through the air under any conditions by purchasing one today!

name: Sphinx
description:
The Sphinx is the ideal disc for newcomers seeking a crossover between a distance and control driver—striking the balance of extended distance without fading prematurely. With its understable release and a manageable speed rating, the Sphinx effortlessly glides with ease, making it perfect for players honing in their accuracy.

For seasoned players, the Sphinx transforms into a valuable, understable utility disc, excelling in hyzer-flips, long anhyzer throws, and powerful rollers. Its versatility ensures its enduring presence in any disc golf bag, proving itself as a reliable and timeless addition for players of all levels. The Sphinx is not just a disc; it's a constant companion that stands the test of time on the course.

name: Sultan
description: The Infinite Discs Sultan is a high-speed distance driver designed for maximum glide and controlled turnover flights. The Sultan offers effortless distance potential for players with the arm speed to handle it. The unique beaded rim provides a secure grip and enhanced torque resistance, helping the disc maintain a smooth, consistent finish even on longer throws. Ideal for shaping shots through the fairway or executing big anhyzer lines, the Sultan brings a versatile option to the distance driver slot.

name: Tomb
description: The TOMB is a flat-topped, low-profile, putt-and-approach disc with a comfortably smooth, beaded rim. The Tomb has just the right amount of stability to cover ground while threading the needle on approach shots, whether thrown forehand or backhand. It can also be used as a crossover mid-range, driving putter, or to hold the line while putting from distance or in moderate winds.

name: Aero
description: Excellent glide and a straight flight path make the Innova Aero a great approach disc. This disc has a wider rim than most putters today. The Aero is a disc with some history. It was the first disc designed specifically for disc golf. It is a putter by today's standards, but before distance driver technology was created, this disc was once thrown 485 feet in competition. This is an old disc, but still a good one.

name: Alien
description: This disc looks like a UFO from the side, which is very fitting for the disc called the Alien. It's got a top like the Sonic, with the classic Fastback-like shape with a thumbtrack and little ridges for grip, and a bottom like a modern midrange. It feels more comfortable than it should, flies like a dream, and might make it in some of our employees' bags.

name: Animal
description: The Animal is a putt & approach disc that has a thumbtrack rim for a solid grip. It is designed to be an approach disc for the backhand or forehand thrower. This has very little glide, which will prevent the disc gliding past the target. Also, it'll be more wind resistant.

name: Ape
description: As a super-overstable high-speed driver, the Innova Ape isn’t made for everyone. Unless you can drive more than 400 feet, this won’t provide maximum distance for backhand throws. The Ape is designed for ultra power throwers, headwind shots, and strategic dog-leg fades. As a power driver, it is available in Star and the durable Champion plastic, in weights from 173-175 grams. This disc is also available in lighter weights in the Blizzard Champion plastic between 130-159g. The lighter weight Blizzard Ape is more suitable for players who can’t throw with ape-like force. This is one of our best selling Blizzard plastic discs.

name: Arachnid
description: The Innova Arachnid is a new midrange disc golf disc.

name: Archangel
description: The Innova Archangel is one of the best discs for newer disc golfers who have trouble controlling fade on their drives. This disc has a good amount of high-speed turn, excellent glide, and limited fade to help young players achieve maximum distance. The Archangel is available in the inexpensive DX plastic and is available in weights from 150 to 175 grams. The highly understable flight characteristics help make this disc a good one for roller shots.

name: Atlas
description: The Atlas is the first disc manufactured by Innova that combines two different plastics in the same disc. This method of utilizing a rim piece that is composed of a different plastic from the flight plate is called an ‘overmold’ and has been very successful for MVP. The rim of the Atlas is composed of a gummy composite which provides unparallelled grip, durability, and consistency in flight. On the underside of the rim there are four ‘compass’ points (hence the Atlas name). These points help secure the outer rim with the inner flight plate.

name: Avatar
description: The Avatar is an overmold midrange disc that combines some of the elements of popular Innova molds like the Roc3, Lion, and Atlas. It is a stable mid with a moderate, controlled fade.

name: Aviar Driver
description: The Aviar Driver is an overstable big bead Aviar putter. This disc is available only in ultra premium Star plastic. The Aviars are the most popular putt and approach discs in the world. This version uses the same mold as the KC and JK Aviars, but with a different grip thanks to the bead.

name: Aviar3
description: The Aviar3 is a modified Aviar putter that is flat-topped and designed for driving and aggressive putts and upshots. It is more overstable than the traditional Aviar and has less glide for a faster flight.

name: AviarX3
description: The AviarX3 is an overstable putt-and-approach disc. This is Innova's most overstable Aviar model to date. The AviarX3 is ideal for headwind upshots and drives. AviarX3 is extremely torque-resistant. It has more high-speed stability than its buddy, the Aviar3.

name: Banshee
description: The Innova Banshee is an overstable fairway driver that is great for fighting the wind. This disc has a strong reliable fade and is good for both backhand and sidearm throws.

name: Beast
description: The Beast is one of the most popular distance drivers in the world, and for good reason. The Innova Beast is a fast, stable driver that isn’t too much for less experienced players. This disc works well for both forehand and backhand throws. While the Beast is a good distance driver for newer players, it’s also used by professionals, including world champion Barry Schultz. This disc works for maximum distance, downwind shots, and turnover throws.

name: Birdie
description: Looking to make more putts? The Birdie is a super-accurate, straight-flying putter. This is a great disc for ultimate frisbee players converting to disc golf. The Birdie is one of the slowest flying discs on the market, but it will fly straight as an arrow. The Birdie is a tall disc and has a very deep rim (not a good disc for forehand throws). It is available in the low-cost DX plastic at a price anyone can afford.

name: Boss
description:
The Innova Boss was the first speed-13 driver on the market. It is one of the more popular ultra-high-speed drivers, and is available in all of Innova's most popular plastics. The world distance record is currently held by David Wiggins Jr. with an R-Pro Boss!

FYI

Most of the plastics the Boss is made in, are stamped as a 13/5/-1/3 flight rating.  However, the champion plastic ones normally fly more overstable, so in many cases the champion will be stamped as a 13/5/0/3 flight rating.

name: Bullfrog
