description: The NICE! is Momemtum Disc Golf's first Fairway Driver. The NICE! is a beginner friendly understable fairway driver that will surf smoothly in the air. For experienced players, this will be a useful utility / understable fairway.

name: Oath
description: The Oath by Negative Disc Golf is a beadless putter with plenty of glide and a consistent stable flight. It’s great for both putting and throwing.

name: Reign
description: The Negative Disc Golf Reign is a flat-topped, overstable midrange built for torque resistance and dependable fade. Designed for controlled approaches and shorter drives, the Reign excels when precision and a strong finish are essential. Its overstability makes it a reliable tool in wind or for players who prefer aggressive lines that demand a confident fade.

name: Ritual
description: The Negative Disc Golf Ritual is a versatile mid-range disc with excellent glide and a predictable fade. Its comfortable profile and consistent flight make it a solid choice for players at all levels, delivering reliable performance on approach shots and controlled drives.

name: Marlin
description: The Neptune Discs Marlin is a very understable fairway driver that should have inherited the name "Flipper". The Marlin will get turn up the fairway, making it awesome for those precision understable shots.

name: Nautilus
description: The Neptune Discs Nautilus is a straight flying midrange disc with a dependable finish. It's a point and shoot midrange that any player would benefit from bagging. The Nautlius is Neptune Discs' quintessential mid.

name: Splash
description: The Neptune Discs Splash is a beadless putter that is perfect for any player. It's a straight flying disc that sails through the air with ease, making it the captain's choice for precision putting.

name: Squid
description: The Neptune Discs Squid is an 11-speed driver designed to cater to various arm speeds. For power arms, it offers a nice flip, while for slower arms, it provides a reliable finish. This versatile disc ensures dependable performance regardless of the player's throwing power.

name: Splinter
description:
The Splinter is a flat-top putter meant for all skill-levels. As an overstable approach disc, this does not turn over when thrown with power.The Splinter works well as a driver, for approach shots, or as a putter. It's quite the capable and versatile mold.

The bottom of the rim is rounded and beadless. The mold offers a comfortable grip to help with consistent releases and accurate throws.

name: Flying Fish Prototype
description:
The Ocean Discs Flying Fish has not passed the PDGA approval process.  Unfortunately the disc design didn’t tick all the boxes.  

From Ocean Discs:

“This disc passed all PDGA tests but the rim configuration and radius gauge tests. Consequently, it cannot be approved at this time. The rim configuration rating was measured at 18.50 kg (PDGA minimum is 26.00), and the leading edge is sharper than the 1/16th-inch radius gauge. Both tests indicate that the disc is significantly too sharp to pass.” @pdga 

Disc golf manufacturers typically don’t advertise or acknowledge these types of mistakes, but they happen more often than you might think. In this case, the inside edge ended up too sharp.

name: Sea Turtle
description: The Sea Turtle is a straight-flying, torque-resistant midrange disc ideal for approach shots with minimal turn. Designed for all skill levels, it handles power from all arm speeds and offers exceptional glide with a small finish. The flight numbers are 5 | 5 | -1 | 1 in Ocean Waste Marine plastic and 5 | 5 | 0 | 1 in premium plastics. PDGA approved for sanctioned events, the Sea Turtle is a reliable go-to disc for accurate midrange shots.

name: Husky
description:
Presenting The Husky, the definitive fairway driver in disc golf. Ideal for players yearning to channel their spirit animal and echo the moon's call with each extensive, effortless hurl. The Husky promises a predictable flight trajectory that'll have you brimming with anticipation on the fairway.

With its balanced glide, The Husky is fitting for both newcomers just beginning to find their footing, and seasoned professionals who've already established their mark in the world of disc golf.

name: Pike
description:
Introducing The Pike, the most aquatic disc on the fairway. This midrange driver caters to both newcomers and experienced professionals, featuring a gentle overturn and exceptional glide for easy, distant flights. But don't be deceived by its placid facade, as Pike embodies the quiet hunter, always prepared to seize every opportunity in its path.

Those with quicker reflexes will witness this disc glide through the air like a quiet predator, combining versatility, dependability, and a dash of sly craftiness, The Pike perfectly encapsulates the ultimate disc golf experience.

name: Santa
description:
Introducing a new disc golf icon: The Santa. Characterized by its stable flight trajectory, Santa offers the ideal solution for direct and precise puts. Its high glide quotient means it needs only the lightest of touches, simplifying your game and making securing the chains a delightful endeavor.

Whether you're a seasoned veteran or just dipping your toes into the sport, Santa proves to be the go-to choice for those seeking a reliable and straightforward disc to add to their collection. Its dependability makes it the perfect choice for players desiring a straight-shooting addition to their disc golf arsenal.

name: Bagger
description: The Bagger is a beaded, overstable putt & approach disc that has a minimal amount of glide. It has a smaller profile compared to other putters and also has a very flat top. This putter provides players with a smooth release from the hand and a strong finish into the basket.

name: Grunt
description: The Grunt is a control driver that does the grunt work for you. For those with a slower arm speed, it is great for backhand/forehand flex shots when needing a bit more distance or for carving out lines through a fairway. Players with a higher arm speed could use this disc for long straight lines with a reliable finish at the end.

name: Spire
description:
Introducing the Spire by Pie Pan Discs, a high-speed distance driver that delivers exceptional distance and control on the disc golf course. Designed to cater to players of all skill levels, the Spire is a reliable driver that can handle a variety of shots with ease.

Available in two distinct plastics, Bakers Blend and Cherry, the Spire offers options to suit different player preferences. The Bakers Blend variant provides a softer and more grippy feel, making it ideal for precise hyzer flips, straight shots, and anhyzer throws. Its excellent grip also ensures confidence in wet weather conditions.

The Cherry Spire offers a stiffer and slightly more stable flight. This variant is perfect for players seeking a disc that can hold consistent hyzer or anhyzer lines. Its increased stability also makes it a reliable choice in windy conditions.

Choose the Spire that best suits your playing style and experience the remarkable performance and versatility that Pie Pan Discs has crafted into this exceptional distance driver.

name: War Dove
description: The Premier Discs' War Dove is a stable, straight-flying putter. It excels when thrown off the tee on dead-straight lines, but it can also be used for all-purpose putting. It's a beadless putter that feels perfect in the hand - not too thin and not too thick. The premium Alpha plastic that this disc comes in takes dye incredibly well.

name: Amulet
description: The Amulet is an moderately overstable midrnage disc that will act as a go-to workhorse for torque resistant approach shots across the fairway.

name: Empire
description: The Prodiscus Empire is a high-speed driver designed for advanced players seeking a beefy, overstable flight. Its thin profile and wide rim offer a comfortable grip and effortless release, allowing for maximum distance and accuracy. With its reliable fade and ability to handle high power throws, the Empire is the perfect disc for conquering any course and building your own disc golf empire.

name: Fasti
description: FASTi lives up to its namesake-- it is a fast driver!  Though rated as a high-speed driver, it is a manageable disc for beginners and does not require massive arm speed. The FASTi has amazing glide and a dependable finishing fade.

name: Jokeri
description: The Jokeri is a popular, highly rated putt & approach disc made by the Finnish disc manufacturer Prodiscus. This is a tall, bulky disc with a flat, almost concave tip. The Jokeri is a very popular disc for short putts as well as long drives. The Prodiscus Jokeri is a reliable, overstable disc that performs very well in windy conditions. This disc is available in both basic and premium plastic.

name: JokeriX
description: The JokeriX is a beaded version of the Jokeri. This is a highly versatile disc that offers excellent control and a comfortable grip. It is a reliable, overstable disc that performs well in a variety of situations.

name: Laseri
description: The Prodiscus Laseri is a neutral disc with a flight rating that is similar to the RESPECTi, but not quite as overstable. With the name like “Laser” you’d expect this disc to be a fast, straight-flyer -- and it delivers.

name: Legenda
description: The Prodiscus Legenda is a thin, ultra-fast driver with a thick rim. This overstable disc is the fastest of the Prodiscus drivers. For big arms, this disc will turn slightly before a long and gradual end-of-flight fade.

name: Midari
description: The Midari is the midrange disc in the Prodiscus line. This stable flyer has a great feel and goes where you want it to go. It is a fairly slow flyer for a midrange, but flies straight, with a sharp end of flight fade. The Prodiscus Midari is available in two plastics. The basic blend is soft and very grippy, while the premium blend is more durable. Midari’s in Premium plastic tend to be more overstable.

name: MidariX
description: The Midari is the midrange disc in the Prodiscus line. This stable flyer has a great feel and goes where you want it to go. It is a fairly slow flyer for a midrange, but flies straight, with a sharp end-of-flight fade. The Prodiscus Midari is available in two plastics. The basic blend is soft and very grippy, while the premium blend is more durable. Midari’s in Premium plastic tend to be more overstable.

name: Origo
description: The Origo, by Prodiscus, is a beadless putt-and-approach disc with a shallower feel in hand. The Origo has a dependably straight flight with minimal fade at the end. This disc is available in both base and premium plastics.

name: Pyramid
description: The Pyramid by Prodiscus is a pro-level stable-to-overstable midrange with a mostly flat profile that is ideal for both forehand and backhand short-range throws. It has a steady finishing fade every time, even when used by experienced power throwers.

name: Razeri
description: The Prodiscus Razeri is a high-speed, overstable driver that allows for very controlled distance shots. The overstable flight makes this disc a great option for forehand throws, as well as very long backhand shots for bigger arms.

name: Respecti
description: The Respecti is an overstable control driver that is great for strategic hooks, forehand throws, and overhand drives. This disc is not recommended for beginners. The Respecti is the first disc that Finnish manufacturer Prodiscus produced.

name: Sparta
description: The Sparta is the second putt and approach disc in the Prodiscus line. This is an Aviar-type putter that you can throw every shot with. Even with low power and moderate spin, the Sparta holds the line nicely.

name: STARi
description: The STARi is an understable mid-range disc from Prodiscus. It's the perfect mid for beginners to gain new distance. The intermediate and advanced players will find the STARi to be amazing for long, understable flights.

name: Talisman
description: The Prodiscus Talisman is a powerful and dependable fairway driver with an overstable flight that inspires confidence in any player's game. Its name, "Talisman," represents the good luck and protection it brings to those who throw it, and with its exceptional control and reliable fade, it's sure to become a treasured addition to your disc golf bag.

name: Troija
description: The Troija is a very overstable approach midrange with a very flat top. It is an excellent choice for powerful forehand throws when you absolutely want a strong end fade, almost fading from initial release. The Troija also works wonderfully on skip angles if you want it to hit the ground and keep fading on the skip. It is a great choice for your bag as an overstable, short-range utility disc.

name: Unicorn
description: Like the mythical animal, the Unicorn is one in a a true rarity! It's a straight-to-stable midrange with only moderate end-fade that can be comfortable thrown either backhand or forehand.

name: Chameleon
description:
Here's what Reptilian's site says about this disc:

"The Chameleon is one of our newest molds. This fairway driver features a perfect combination of turn and fade, making for a versatile workhorse. Featuring a 10 speed rim with a blunt profile, the Chameleon flies slower than the wing suggests, providing a chunky grip and a very unique speed. 

The Chameleon turns gently and finishes with a reliable late fade."

name: Gila
description: The Gila is the midrange for players that want a quicker, more overstable option than traditional mids. Having a large bead & flat top, the Gila will speedily become your most used disc.

name: Iguana
description: The Iguana flies like a Roc/Comet/Gila hybrid. It has moderate turn at high speed and it has an equally moderate fade when it slows down. It sort of reminds of us a slower Chameleon.

name: Komodo
description:
Designed with a rounded shoulder, flat top, and micro-bead, the Komodo boasts an aggressive flight path and exceptional stability.

This beefy utility driver not only handles headwinds with ease but also excels in executing high skipping hyzers and impressive force flex lines. Experience its unique feel and unleash its power on the course!

name: Scale
description: The Reptilian Disc Golf Scale is a very reliable, overstable putter. It feels very similar to the Wizard, and has a subtle thumb track around the top. It has gained a lot of popularity since it's creation. "2015 Disc of the Year - 4th Place, Highest Placing Putter of 2015"

name: Arrowhead
description: Just as arrowheads have guided various cultures throughout history, the Sacred Discs Arrowhead midrange is your modern-day compass on the disc golf course. Designed to steer you down the straight and narrow path to your target, this disc offers both functionality and a nod to the rich legacy of arrowheads, which not only served as tools but also hold cultural and historical significance as artifacts.

name: Oracle
description:
Experience precision on the fairway with the Sacred Discs Oracle, a versatile disc crafted for easy control, whether you're executing a hyzer-flip or threading through narrow fairways. Its sleek profile ensures agile navigation, and it's engineered to deliver consistently neutral flight, making it a perfect fit for players of all skill levels.

In the world of Greek mythology, the Oracle represents various sources of divine insight. The prophecies whispered by the Oracle were held in high esteem, with kings and leaders frequently seeking her guidance when confronted with pivotal decisions.

name: Lucky 13
description: The Lucky 13 is arguably one of the most understable drivers on the market. Upon release the disc turns sharply in an anhyzer angle, sails far with that flight angle, and then gradually fades back to the ground. With some fine tuning, this is a great choice for practiced disc golfers who want some easy distance. For players with a strong snap, this disc will likely be too understable.

name: Medusa
description: The Medusa by Skyquest is a long distance disc with a perfect mix of speed, glide, turn and fade for newer disc golfers. This disc is fast! It maintains its speed courtesy of its low profile and thick rim. The flight rating for the Medusa is 11/5/-2/2. Unlike the L13, which shows its understability from launch, the Medusa gently turns, maintains a straight line, and then gently fades to its landing destination.

name: Cardinal
description: The Stokely Discs Cardinal, a mid-range disc, is designed to provide a reliable flight pattern that benefits players during approaches and short drives. Initially, it exhibits a slight overstable trajectory, which stabilizes early in its flight. As it progresses, the Cardinal demonstrates a strong fade towards the end, which is particularly effective in preventing the disc from overshooting the target. This disc is adept at maintaining its flight angle, even in the face of strong headwinds, ensuring consistent performance when precision is crucial.

name: Finch
description: The Stokley Discs Finch is a beaded putter built for control and stability. It shares much of the Wren’s straight, predictable flight but adds a touch more fade and overall stability. The Finch holds lines well in any condition, making it a dependable throwing or putting option for players who prefer a firmer, more stable feel with a bead.

name: Lark
description: The Lark Fairway driver by Stokely Discs is a beginner friendly disc. Created to fly straight with a slight fade at the end, this disc is perfect for anyone who likes a slower speed disc that maintains its steady flight path.

name: Owl
description: The Stokely Discs Owl is a slow, overstable approach disc designed for short to medium distances. Its exceptional torque resistance makes it especially effective for sidearm throws, and its low profile helps it maintain stability in all wind conditions. With a reliable ability to stop quickly on the green, the Owl is the perfect tool for precision approaches.

name: Peregrine
description: The Stokely Peregrine is an overstable high-speed driver made for long hyzers, wind resistance, and consistent flight at full power.

name: Robin
description: The Stokley Discs Robin is a fairway driver designed for straight, controlled flights. It features minimal turn and a gentle, consistent fade that makes it easy to handle for players of all skill levels. The Robin offers dependable precision and versatility, serving as a reliable all-purpose driver on any course.

name: Wren
description: The Stokely Discs Wren, a beadless putter, excels in its flight performance, delivering a straight path at slow speeds which reduces the likelihood of missed putts. This predictable trajectory ensures accurate putting with minimal deviation. When thrown with more force, it slightly flips up before stabilizing and concluding with a slow, manageable fade. This behavior allows for precise distance control, making the Wren an ideal choice for players seeking reliability and consistency in their putting and approach game.

name: Abyss
description: Enter the Abyss with this impressive disc from Storm Disc Golf. This is a workhorse on the fairways with a manageable speed and dependable fade for consistent performance under all conditions.

name: Radar
description:
The Radar is a stable putter with impressive glide. It has a very dependable flight and is great for not only putting but also for driving off the tee. 

If you are looking for your next disc that will seek out the chains look no further than the Radar!

name: The Eye
description: The Eye is a straight-flying midrange designed to fit into anyone's bag. For the advanced player and those who can throw hard, this will result in a slightly understable disc. For a beginner or intermediate player with lower arm speed, this will be a stable disc that can hold most lines.

name: Wall Cloud
description: The Wall Cloud is a unique midrange disc featuring a "double bead" on the bottom of the disc. It's an overstable midrange, that will hold straight for a while before reliably fading out at the end of the flight.

name: Canyon
description: The Canyon by Trash Panda is an overstable midrange that is made in the USA out of recycled plastic. Just like its namesake, the Canyon was shaped by time. It is a dependable midrange with a comfortable rim configuration that can handle the throwing styles of newer and experienced players. An overstable midrange that holds steady in the wind and fades when you need it to.

name: Dune
description:
The Dune was created under the ideas "Little by little a little becomes a lot" and "it's not about someone doing everything, it's about everyone doing something"

The Dune was created by Trash Panda in collaboration with fans, the YouTube community, and many people along the way tring to make a difference in the world by creating a perfect midrange. This disc is designed to be used for every shot. It's a straight flying, glidey, dependable workhorse. Anyone can throw and love this disc. 

Grow the sport sustainably!

name: Inner Core
description:
Trash Panda's goal in making the Inner Core was to make a premium putter that would fly straight for everybody without using a single additive, and while remaining 100% recycled and recyclable. 

The color of any particular run of the Inner Core is based on whatever the regrind / trash was we received for the plastic blend. The color chooses the disc, rather than using additives to color the disc.

The Inner Core does not have a hot stamp because of the waste that is created in the stamping process. This leaves the surface clean without hurting the environment.

name: Outer Core
description: The Outer Core by Trash Panda is a beaded version of the Inner Core putter, adding a more stable flight (more fade) and a slightly deeper rim profile.

name: Ozone
description: The Ozone is an understable, flippy fairway driver by Trash Panda, made in their usual recycled plastic. It is a great disc choice for players who have slower arm speeds or who like reaching respectable distances without a lot of power.

name: Procyon
description: The Procyon is a distance driver from Trash Panda Disc Golf that is made out of 100% recycled plastic material. It is designed to be a distance driver for the masses with a popular 12-speed rim and not too much turn or fade. It can be a key tool in any player's bag as they develop their disc golf game while choosing discs that are environmentally responsible.

name: Zeal
description:
The ZEAL is a putter released by Uplay Disc Golf Foundation to be an easy-to-throw, comfortable putter for players of all levels and abilities. It is a beadless putter with a rounded rim for maximum comfort when using it to throw on approach shots or as a putter at the basket. It holds a very straight flight path, resisting an unwanted fade whether thrown long or short. 

Every Uplay disc sold, including the ZEAL putter, helps to raise funds for the Uplay Disc Golf Foundation's mission of growing the sport of disc golf in communities. They teach instructors and students in physical education programs, in elementary schools, middle schools, and high schools everywhere.

name: Axe
description: The Viking Axe is a midrange disc, but with a slow glidey flight like a putter. The Axe has a slight dome and a stable flight with a fading finish. This is an excellent precision disc when you need an accurate placement.

name: Barbarian
description: The Barbarian fills the Viking Discs's slot of stable fairway driver. The Barbarian flies straight and true, before reliably fading at the end of the flight. Use this trustworthy disc in wooded and open courses alike!

name: Berserker
description: The Viking Discs Berserker is an understable driver that is excellent for beginners looking for more distance. The Berserker works well for hyzer flip shots and can help you improve your throwing technique.

name: Cosmos
description: The Viking Cosmos is described as a point and shoot fairway driver. This is a straight-flying driver that is great for beginners and works great for tight courses where you need to hit the tiny gaps.

name: Fenrir
description: The Fenrir is an overstable distance driver. This 10-speed driver makes for a reliable distance driver. This disc will continue to fly straight in most windy conditions, making this a great disc for hyzer throws and as a reliable wind disc.

name: Knife
description: The Viking Knife putt & approach disc is slightly overstable and can handle a lot of power. The rim is designed to be comfortable with backhand throws using both a power and fan grip. It is a beadless putter with a blunt rounded feel.

name: Loki
description: The Viking Discs Loki is a wide-diameter "Utility" Putt and Approach disc. With a fade of 4, this disc is sure to hook up just when you need it to. This disc is hard to flip over, even in a headwind. The Loki's specialty is short distance approaches when you need a reliable, hard fade.

name: Nordic Warrior
description: The Nordic Warrior is a very stable midrange that can take a lot of power and still fade back at the end of the flight. Viking claims that this disc is "not overstable, but you can really feel the control on big hyzers and windy situations." This mid is comfortable for, and works well for both backhand and forehand throws.

name: Odin
description: The Odin is an overstable, high-speed distance driver. This distance driver provides sufficient stability to handle power and maintain its course. This will fly straight and exhibit a little turn right before it fades out for the finish.

name: Ragnarok
description: The Viking Ragnarok is a fast driver with a very manageable rim size. This disc is designed to be a long control driver that will provide S-curve flights and fading finishes. Depending on your release angle, the Ragnarok can be useful in all kinds of different situations.

name: Rune
description: The Viking Discs Rune is a low-profile putt & approach disc with a perfectly straight flight. This putter is beaded, which helps it maintain stability with wear over time. The Rune is great for putting and has an easy to predict flight path.

name: Thunder God Thor
description: The Viking Thunder God Thor is a very fast distance driver with an ultra-wide rim. This is a driver that you can trust to fade out at the end of the flight, even if released at an anhyzer angle. Advanced players looking for maximum distance will want to try throwing the Thunder God Thor.

name: Valhalla
description: The Valhalla is a stable distance driver. It has a great mix of glide and stability to hold up on powerful throws. This disc has been described as "effortless to point, shoot and fade". Get extra distance with less effort by throwing a Valhalla.

name: Angler
description: The Wild Discs Angler is an overstable utility midrange. Allowing you to shape all kind of unique shots. This proves to be a useful tool whether it is getting around a bend, or getting out of a tricky situation. This disc is meant to fly straight and then fade hard. Allowing for flex shots, hyzers, or dog-leg shots.

name: Great White
description: The Great White, from Wild Discs, is a high-speed, overstable distance driver. This driver has high glide, allowing it to achieve great distance and long flights. This is a dependable disc that can handle power, making it an excellent distance driver for those power throwers, or as a utility disc.

name: Hyena
description: This is a great understable farway driver from Wild Discs. It is very straight with great hyzer flip capability.

name: Tasmanian Devil V2
description: The Wild Discs Tasmanian Devil V2 is a redesigned flatter version of the Tasmanian Devil. This overstable fairway driver can handle even the strongest of headwinds. It is an excellent overstable utility driver and a pre-level, high-performance disc for power throwers.

name: Dream
description: The Dream, by Wing It Disc Golf, is a wide-rim distance driver, but with enough turn to be very favorable to players with low arm speed. New players, young players, or those who typically can't throw a high-speed driver will find that the Dream avoids a premature fade and flies well for them even with the wide rim. Wing It Disc Golf continues to release discs that are great additions to the bags of recreational players as well as for experienced players who like discs that flip-up and hold the line.

name: Pursue
description: Are you ready for an overstable midrange from Wing It? We already know Pursue is going to have a great feel based on the other discs they've released so far. It looks to be overstable, a little bit on the deeper end, and it comes in Wing plastic with those beautiful colors we all love. Other than that, there's not much we know so far. It was approved on 5/29/23

name: Aqu
description: The Aqu (or Aquarius) by X-Com is an understable midrange disc that is perfect for new players. It has plenty of glide and minimal fade, making it a great choice for slower arm speeds.

name: Ari
description: The Ari is an overstable fairway / control driver by XCom that can handle power and finishes with a solid end-fade. It's a good choice for powerful backhand or forehand throws.

name: Bennu
description:
The Bennu is a comfortable, straight-flying putter. It is beadless and has a low-profile rim similar to popular understable putters like the Deputy or Fierce. It's an ideal putter for new players and experienced players alike. 

NOTE: This disc is PDGA approved as the "XPT1" or "Globefish"

name: Cetus
description: The Cetus is a straight-flying fairway driver for all players, delivering maximum control without needing a lot of arm-speed or power.

name: Dra
description: The Dra (or Draco) is a stable 12-speed distance driver that is an excellent choice for intermediate and experienced players seeking to maximize their throw distance.

name: Eden
description:
The Eden is a stable midrange that you can throw for a strong approach to the basket. This disc finishes with a little fade after a straight flight. The rim is smooth and has a low profile for comfortable handling on controlled shots.

NOTE: This disc is PDGA approved as the "XPT2" or "Dolphin"

name: Gem
description: The Gem (or Gemini) by XCom is a neutral, straight-flying midrange that can handle enough torque to work well for seasoned players as well as new players. It has a rim that almost looks like a fairway crossover disc because of it's aerodynamic design.

name: Griffon
description:
The Griffon is an easy-to-throw, understable midrange disc that is ideal for new players or for anhyzers and shots that you want to finish right. If you're a new player with throws that seem to fade too early every time, a disc like this might help you get that throw straightened out.

NOTE: This disc is PDGA approved under the name "XPT3" or "Grampus"

name: Helios
description:
The Helios is an understable, easy-to-throw fairway driver for general purpose use on the disc golf course. Beginners will find the Helios a very suitable disc to help straighten their throws for better distance under low power. 

NOTE: This disc is PDGA approved as "XPT4" or the "Sailfish"

name: Leo
description: The Leo by XCom is an understable distance driver, making it a good choice for players who enjoy throwing high-speed discs, but who may not have a lot of throwing power.

name: Libra
description: The Libra by XCom Discs is a neutral speed-11 distance driver with only a slight end-fade. It is a great choice for intermediate players who want a controllable distance driver for quality flight and not too much side-to-side movement.

name: Ophiuchus
description: The Ophiuchus by XCom is a very easy-to-throw fairway driver that works great for beginners. It is understable enough to make a good flip-up or roller disc for seasoned players and is a fun option to have in your bag.

name: Pisces
description: The Pisces by XCom is a workhorse control driver with a slightly overstable fade. It can be a go-to for many shorter drivers where control is more important than distance.

name: Tau
description: The Tau or "Taurus" putter by XCom is a straight-flying putter for maximum accuracy when finishing at the basket.

name: Volans
description: The Volans by XCom is a 9-speed driver for excellent flight results that balance distance with controlled precision. It feels great for backhand and forehand throwers.

name: Zion Driver
description:
The Zion driver is a general-purpose distance driver that can be thrown by beginner to intermediate players with great results. There's enough stability to handle moderate power and it works great for flex shots that take an S-curve path across the fairway. 

NOTE: This disc mold is PDGA approved as the "XPT5" or "Volador"

name: Kyrios
description: The Kyrios is a glidey point-and-shoot disc with a neutral to slightly stable flight. It has slightly shallow rim depth which makes for a comfortable hand feel. The extra glide makes it perfect for shots off the tee, and its neutral flight will help master approach shots that require precision.

name: Petra
description: The Petra by Yeet Street is an all-purpose midrange disc that holds the line with only a slight end-fade. It can be used to manage shorter distance shots with tight fairways that require precision flight.

name: Bai Ze
description: Bai Ze, a revered mythical creature in ancient Chinese lore that possesses the extraordinary ability to speak, understand all things, and know the secrets of spirits and gods. This fairway driver can be used by all players without difficulty to navigate the course with ease.

name: Bi
description: The Yikun Bi is a stable-flying fairway driver with a good feel and adequate speed. This is a favorite among Yikun sponsored players. The Bi was approved by the PDGA on December 13, 2021.

name: Claws
description: The Claws is an amazing straight-flying putt & approach disc. This slow-flying putter gently floats through the air and lands exactly where you want it to. This is a tall disc with a super blunt rim and thumb grip grooves on top.

name: Crossbow
description: The Crossbow (Nu) is designed to be a utility disc for upshots and strong headwinds. This reliable mid has minimal glide and very strong fade. You won't be able to flip this disc over! It is meant to drop in on the target with overstable precision.

name: DA'E
description: The Yikun Da’E is an ultra-fast, overstable driver. This is an excellent disc for advanced players looking for max distance, and works very well for forehand drives. The Da’E has a very wide rim and is the best wind fighter in the Yikun line.

name: FU
description: The FU, by Yikun, is an all-purpose fairway driver that features a manageable "speed-7" rim and a consistent end fade. It's a great disc for players at all levels looking for control when navigating the fairway.

name: Fu Sang
description: The Yikun Fu Shang brings stability and consistency to your approach and putt game. Its flat top profile and outstanding appearance will capture the eye. Experience premium performance every time you throw with this brilliant, colorful disc. With the Yikun Fu Sang you get worry-free stability from tee to green.

name: Fu Xi
description: The Fu Xi by Yikun is a quality distance driver with only slight end-fade for a fairly neutral flight. It also has excellent glide for a good forward push at the end of its flight.

name: Gou
description: The Gou (Chinese for Sky Dog) is a very understable fairway driver that is ideal for beginners. If you're looking for something that will get easy distance this is it. For more experienced players, the Gou is great for straight-line hyzer flip shots.

name: Gui
description: The Gui is a beadless overstable putter with a flat top, deep rim, and unique slip-resistant grip plates on the bottom flight plate. This is a very comfortable putt & approach disc that is very similar to the Discmania Tiger Warrior.

name: Hammer (Chui)
description: The Yikun Hammer (Chui) is a utility putter designed for putting on windy days (or any day). This is a slow, overstable putter - perfect for hyzer putters or short approaches. This disc features a raised rim and an inner rim thumbtrack on the top of the disc, creating essentially, two raised thumbtracks. (See picture!)

name: Hou Yi
description: The Hou Yi is a long-range disc with an outstanding gliding path, featuring a well-suited disc rim width and precise flight characteristics, making it an ideal choice for players across all skill levels. Whether you're a seasoned competitor or a beginner, Hou Yi stands out as a must-have option, especially if you're seeking an all-around long-range disc for various levels of competition.

name: Hu
description: The Yikun Hu (Chinese Nine Tailed Fox) is a slightly understable control driver that is slightly faster, and more stable than the Gou. This is a great all-purpose driver.

name: Jiao
description:
The Jiao is an understable fairway driver, designed with the beginning disc golf player in mind, as well as being perfect for any thrower with lower arm speed.  This is one of, if not the most understable PDGA driver on the market. The Jiao can achieve peak distance with less effort and is also a great place to start for young players. This driver is designed to provide more distance for disc golfers throwing less than 250 feet. The Jiao will provide disc golf flight with a throw style used for Ultimate Frisbee.

More experienced players can use the Jiao for easy rollers or low-power hyzer-flip shots and quick turning anhyzer throws. If you're looking for a great first driver for your first ever round of disc golf, this is it.

name: Jun
description: The Jun is a fast, understable/stable distance driver that is great for intermediate disc golfers. If you’re looking for straight-line distance on a back hand throw, the Jun performs.

name: Kang
description: The Kang is a beaded, low-speed, high-glide, high-turn, and no-fade midrange that provides an understable flight. It is capable of great hyzer-flip lines, anhyzer lines, and easy, touch approach shots.

name: Kui
description: The KUI, from Yikun Discs, is a high-quality midrange with a comfortable rim and domey profile for high glide and a precise, controlled flight. It has a consistent end-fade, allowing you to approach the basket with confidence, but it can also be thrown with power without flipping over.

name: Ling
description: The Ling is an understable putter that features a textured portion on the face plate. With a shallow rim and a blunt nose, this disc provides a comfortable grip for smaller and medium-sized hands. It is also an easy-to-throw disc, which works excellently as a first putter or as a disc to use in order to learn how to control a disc's angle.

name: Long
description: This stable distance driver is an excellent introductory disc for players moving from lower-speed drivers to higher speed distance drivers. It has a good dome to it and a comfortable rim, providing a comfortable disc that can be thrown with confidence. Flying through the air it'll provide high glide, high-speed turn, and a good fade at the end.

name: Luan
description: The LU or LUAN is a wide-rimmed, high-speed driver designed to be a bomber for intermediate and experienced players. With slight understability upon release and a predictable, prominent fade, it will draw a beautiful S-curve through the sky as it maximizes your distance potential.

name: Meteor Hammer
description: The Meteor Hammer is a double-overmold putter. The double-overmold discs are a Yikun specialty! The Meteor Hammer is not like its counterpart, the Hammer (Chui). The Meteor Hammer is a rounded putter that flies dead straight to slightly understable. It's really nice for putting and driving alike, if you take a little heat off of the drives.

name: Ne Zha
description: Zha is a celestial being in ancient Chinese mythology and legends, and the disc with his namesake is also a thing of legend. With an easily managed, understable flight, it can hold an anhyzer line and resist fading for new players and experienced players who want to manage a tight fairway with minimal power to control the flight path.

name: Nv Wa
description: The Nv Wa by Yikun Discs is an understable fairway driver that is easy to control for all players who want to shot-shape with minimal end fade.

name: QI
description: The QI is a high-speed, wide-rim driver that is designed for a neutral flight. While it has some turn and fade, the net result should have the disc finish straight ahead. It's a great way to test your throwing speed and see if you manage a speed-13 and have it finish straight ahead on the fairway for maximum distance. The QI also has great glide to get those extra few feet as it glides to the ground.

name: Rong
description: The Rong is a high-speed driver that exhibits understable flight patterns. This wide-rimmed driver provides a comfortable grip to help achieve long distances. Its flight will show a high speed turn with minimal fade, providing an excellent first high-speed distance driver, or a roller, or a utility turnover disc.

name: She Gong
description: The She Gong is a maximum distance driver by Yikun. This mold is manufactured in Yikun's prettiest plastic to date, GLAZE plastic. The flight numbers say 13/5/0/2. However, for players with more power, the She Gong will hyzer flip and ride for impressive distances. For those players with less power, this disc could be the go-to, straight to fade, distance annihilator. PDGA Approved on 1/30/23

name: Shu
description: The SHU is an interesting midrange that has a shape like a low-speed driver with a sharper outer rim. The rim, however, is still narrow enough to be a slow-speed flyer, meaning that players do not need power to throw the SHU. It is a great beginner disc that will feel like a very easy-to-throw driver at a midrange speed. Its understable design means that it will act resistant to fade for new players.

name: Tomahawk
description: The Tomahawk is a straight-flying, deadly accurate weapon of choice on the fairway when thrown by beginners or at low speeds. Advanced players will find the Tomahawk to be very understable. The double-overmold rim in the Tortoise Line provides a great look as well as a great grip for a perfect release.

name: Twin Swords
description: The Twin Swords is the first roll-out disc model from Yikun in their Tortoise Line plastic which is their overmold "Shell" configuration. The rim is attached to the flight plate through their own, unique method, giving the discs an amazing look. The Twin Swords is a distance driver with a Speed-12 rating and wide, yet comfortable rim width. The Twin Swords is a unique and exciting offering to the disc golf world, earning a spot in the bag for players at all levels.

name: View
description: The Yikun View is the beginner-friendly fairway driver in the Yikun lineup. This easy-to-throw fairway driver is perfect for straight line control and hyzer flip shots. In inexpensive Tiger Line plastic, the View is affordable for any budget. For long-lasting performance, try the View in the Phoenix or Dragon Lines.

name: Wei
description: The Wei is an ultra-understable moderately high-speed distance driver from Yikun. It has an understable flight pattern that is similar to the Gou. It features a nice, predictable fade at the end of its flight, affording great distance to both new and experienced players with its wide rim and great glide. For disc golfers who loved the no-longer-available Vibram unLace, this is a great substitute.

name: Wings
description: The Yikun Wings is a unique golf disc that serves as sort of a midrange and putter hybrid. This utility wedge is designed to provide accurate upshots, without overshooting the basket. The Wings is a low-cost disc available in Yikun’s inexpensive Tiger-Line plastic. For a midrange, the Wings has a very thin profile and rim. It has a slightly overstable flight path and provides a unique throwing experience. The Wings is very similar to the Discmania Spring Ox.

name: Wu Kong
description: Wu Kong is a major Chinese supernatural, mythical character who was born from a celestial stone that emerged during the creation of the world. This distance driver is a powerful, premium disc that summons the spirit of Wu Kong, delivering high performance and almost mythically powerful distance results for those who dare wield its power. It is not too fast and not too overstable, but definitely dependable on the course.

name: Xi He
description: The Xi He is an understable 9-speed driver which is an excellent choice for newer players or for people with slower arm speeds that want to transition to a longer-distance driver. It has a lot of turn to keep the disc from fading early.

name: Xing
description: The Xing is a comfortable, beaded putter that is fine-tuned for putting accuracy. It has a similar feel to the popular Judge or Wizard putter with a slightly straighter flight.

name: Yan
description: The YAN is a slower mid-range disc with a deep rim that feels almost like a large putter. It flies dead straight with even a light touch and minimal power. It's a great, glidey disc for approach shots straight up the gut to the basket.

name: Yao
description: The Yikun Yao is a solid midrange disc available in multiple plastic types. This disc has an excellent feel and is a solid, all-around mid with moderate end fade.

name: Yi
description: The Yi is a straight-flying, high-glide putter, providing a very versatile disc whether throwing with force or as a putter. It has a rounded nose, and beadless rim that makes for a good, comfortable grip whether in a fan grip or a power grip. This straight-flying disc can prove to be a great go-to putter in all circumstances.

name: Zheng
description: The Yikun Zheng is a versatile midrange capable of holding a variety of lines. This is capable of holding a hyzer line and providing a solid flex line. It can also hold an anhyzer line, making it a solid, go-to midrange.

name: Zhu
description: The Zhu, from Yikun Discs, is a manageable fairway driver with a slight end-fade. It was designed for release in Yikun's "Swift" plastic which is more lightweight and floats in water. This makes it a good disc for beginners or those who fear nearby water hazards. If you're looking for a floating disc golf driver, try the Yikun Zhu.
